
```{r install_packages, echo=FALSE, include=FALSE}
# List of required CRAN packages
required_cran <- c(
  "ggpubr",
  "ggfortify",
  "RColorConesa",
  "UpSetR",
  "tidyverse",
  "cowplot",
  "ComplexUpset",
  "ggplot2",
  "gtools",
  "scales",
  "forcats",
  "patchwork"
)

# Install missing CRAN packages
missing_cran <- required_cran[!required_cran %in% installed.packages()[, "Package"]]
if (length(missing_cran) > 0) {
  message("Installing missing CRAN packages: ", paste(missing_cran, collapse = ", "))
  install.packages(missing_cran, repos = "https://cloud.r-project.org")
}

# Bioconductor packages (NOISeq)
if (!"NOISeq" %in% installed.packages()[, "Package"]) {
  message("Installing NOISeq from Bioconductor...")
  if (!requireNamespace("BiocManager", quietly = TRUE)) {
    install.packages("BiocManager", repos = "https://cloud.r-project.org")
  }
  BiocManager::install("NOISeq", ask = FALSE)
}
```

```{r load_libraries, echo=FALSE}
# Load packages
library(ggpubr)
library(ggfortify)
library(NOISeq)
library(RColorConesa)
library(grid)
library(UpSetR)
library(tidyverse)
library(cowplot)
library(ComplexUpset)
library(ggplot2)
library(gtools)
library(scales)
library(forcats)
library(patchwork)

r_source_dir <- if (dir.exists("R")) "R" else "src/plotting/R"
source(file.path(r_source_dir, "00_figure_config.R"))
source(file.path(r_source_dir, "02_themes.R"))
source(file.path(r_source_dir, "08_read_level_plots.R"))
source(file.path(r_source_dir, "10_ref_tusco_sirv_violins.R"))
out_dir <- plot_creation_dir()
```

```{r read_sqanti_stacked_bar, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# Read multiple classification files (helpers + reusable pipeline)
base_dir <- paper_sqanti_reads_root()
pacbio_dir <- file.path(base_dir, "pacbio")
ont_dir    <- file.path(base_dir, "ont")

cat.palette <- read_level_structural_cat_palette
category_map <- read_level_structural_category_map
display_levels <- read_level_mouse_display_levels

# Helper: read one file, summarize, and discard raw data immediately
summarize_sqanti <- function(dir_path, file_vec, label_vec, tech_value) {
  purrr::map2_dfr(
    file_vec,
    label_vec,
    ~ {
      path <- file.path(dir_path, .x)
      if (!file.exists(path)) {
        return(tibble::tibble(
          category_label = character(),
          num_reads = double(),
          sample = character(),
          technology = character()
        ))
      }
      df <- readr::read_tsv(path, show_col_types = FALSE, col_select = "structural_category")
      result <- df %>%
        dplyr::mutate(structural_category = as.character(.data$structural_category)) %>%
        dplyr::transmute(category_label = dplyr::recode(.data$structural_category, !!!category_map)) %>%
        dplyr::filter(!is.na(category_label)) %>%
        dplyr::count(category_label, name = "num_reads") %>%
        dplyr::mutate(sample = .y, technology = tech_value)
      rm(df)
      gc()
      result
    }
  )
}

# Build aggregated table for a tissue (prefix 'B'/'K', label base 'Brain'/'Kidney')
build_category_counts <- function(prefix, label_base, read_numbers) {
  sample_labels <- paste(label_base, 1:5)
  sample_codes  <- paste0(prefix, 31:35)
  pacbio_files  <- paste0(prefix, 31:35, "_reads_classification.txt")
  ont_files     <- paste0(prefix, 31:35, "_classification.txt")

  pb_paths <- file.path(pacbio_dir, pacbio_files)
  ont_paths <- file.path(ont_dir, ont_files)
  missing_pb <- pb_paths[!file.exists(pb_paths)]
  missing_ont <- ont_paths[!file.exists(ont_paths)]
  if (length(missing_pb) > 0L || length(missing_ont) > 0L) {
    stop(
      "SQANTI-reads classification files not found.\n",
      "  pacbio_dir: ", pacbio_dir, "\n",
      "  ont_dir: ", ont_dir, "\n",
      if (length(missing_pb) > 0L) {
        paste0("  First missing PacBio file: ", missing_pb[1L], "\n")
      } else "",
      if (length(missing_ont) > 0L) {
        paste0("  First missing ONT file: ", missing_ont[1L], "\n")
      } else "",
      "Set JOIN_AND_CALL_NIH_DATA if data live outside paper_nih_data_root().",
      call. = FALSE
    )
  }

  counts <- dplyr::bind_rows(
    summarize_sqanti(pacbio_dir, pacbio_files, sample_labels, "pacbio"),
    summarize_sqanti(ont_dir,    ont_files,    sample_labels, "ont")
  )
  if (nrow(counts) == 0L) {
    stop("No classified reads after loading SQANTI files.", call. = FALSE)
  }

  rn <- read_numbers %>%
    dplyr::filter(sample %in% sample_codes) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(sample_label = paste(label_base, as.integer(stringr::str_sub(sample, -1, -1))))

  assigned <- counts %>%
    dplyr::group_by(sample, technology) %>%
    dplyr::summarise(assigned = sum(num_reads), .groups = "drop")

  unaligned <- dplyr::bind_rows(
    rn %>% dplyr::transmute(sample = sample_label, technology = "pacbio", total = pb_fastq),
    rn %>% dplyr::transmute(sample = sample_label, technology = "ont",    total = ont_fastq)
  ) %>%
    dplyr::left_join(assigned, by = c("sample", "technology")) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      assigned   = dplyr::coalesce(assigned, 0),
      num_reads  = pmax(0, total - assigned),
      category_label = "Unaligned"
    ) %>%
    dplyr::select(category_label, num_reads, sample, technology)

  dplyr::bind_rows(counts, unaligned) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      category_label = factor(category_label, levels = display_levels),
      sample = factor(sample, levels = sample_labels),
      technology = factor(technology, levels = c("pacbio","ont"))
    ) %>%
    dplyr::arrange(dplyr::desc(num_reads))
}

plot_sqanti_faceted <- function(counts_df, title = NULL) {
  plot_read_level_sqanti_stacked(
    counts_df,
    display_levels = display_levels,
    title = title,
    include_unstranded = FALSE,
    show_technology_axis = TRUE
  )
}

```

```{r build_category_counts, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}

read_number_file <- file.path(base_dir, "read_numbers_joint.tsv")
read_numbers <- read.table(read_number_file, header = TRUE, sep = "\t")

category_counts_brain  <- build_category_counts("B", "Brain", read_numbers)
category_counts_kidney <- build_category_counts("K", "Kidney", read_numbers)

sqanti_assigned_reads <- function(counts_df) {
  sum(
    counts_df$num_reads[as.character(counts_df$category_label) != "Unaligned"],
    na.rm = TRUE
  )
}

message(
  "Brain SQANTI assigned reads (non-Unaligned): ",
  format(sqanti_assigned_reads(category_counts_brain), big.mark = ",")
)
if (sqanti_assigned_reads(category_counts_brain) <= 0) {
  stop(
    "All brain reads are Unaligned (white bars on white panels). ",
    "Check SQANTI classification paths and category_map.",
    call. = FALSE
  )
}

```


```{r plot_sqanti_stacked_bar, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}

# # Back-compat for the earlier single-bar-by-sample plot: collapse across technology
# category_counts <- category_counts_brain %>%
#   dplyr::group_by(sample, category_label) %>%
#   dplyr::summarise(num_reads = sum(num_reads), .groups = "drop")

# # Single stacked barplot
# ggplot(category_counts, aes(x = sample, y = num_reads, fill = category_label)) +
#   geom_bar(stat = "identity", width = 0.8, color = "black") +
#   scale_fill_manual(
#     values = cat.palette,
#     breaks = display_levels,
#     labels = c(
#       "Full\nSplice Match",
#       "Incomplete\nSplice Match",
#       "Novel\nIn Catalog",
#       "Novel Not\nIn Catalog",
#       "Genic\nGenomic",
#       "Antisense",
#       "Fusion",
#       "Intergenic",
#       "Genic\nIntron"
#     )
#   ) +
#   labs(x = NULL, y = "Number of reads", fill = "Structural Category") +
#   theme(
#     axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
#     panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
#     panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank()
#   )

```


```{r plot_sqanti_faceted_line, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}

# One-row faceted plot: five boxes (Brain 1–5), two bars per box (PacBio, ONT)
brain_sq_reads_plot <- plot_sqanti_faceted(category_counts_brain, title = "Brain")
brain_sq_reads_plot
paper_ggsave(file.path(out_dir, "brain_sq_reads_plot.pdf"), plot = brain_sq_reads_plot, size = "mouse_10panel")

# Kidney plot using same functions
kidney_sq_reads_plot <- plot_sqanti_faceted(category_counts_kidney, title = "Kidney")
kidney_sq_reads_plot
paper_ggsave(file.path(out_dir, "kidney_sq_reads_plot.pdf"), plot = kidney_sq_reads_plot, size = "mouse_10panel")

```

```{r plot_x, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}

read_number_file <- file.path(base_dir, "read_numbers_joint.tsv")
read_numbers <- read.table(read_number_file, header = TRUE, sep = "\t")

read_numbers

```


```{r plot_read_numbers_faceted, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}

# Utilities for read_numbers plotting

# Infer sample label (Brain/Kidney 1..5) from sample code like B31..B35 or K31..K35
infer_sample_label <- function(sample_code) {
  idx <- as.integer(stringr::str_sub(sample_code, -1, -1))
  tissue <- dplyr::case_when(
    stringr::str_starts(sample_code, "B") ~ "Brain",
    stringr::str_starts(sample_code, "K") ~ "Kidney",
    TRUE ~ "Sample"
  )
  paste(tissue, idx)
}

# Build tidy counts for a tissue from read_numbers
build_readnum_counts <- function(read_numbers, tissue = c("Brain", "Kidney")) {
  tissue <- match.arg(tissue)

  required_cols <- c("sample", "ont_fastq", "ont_prim_aln", "pb_fastq", "pb_prim_aln")
  if (!all(required_cols %in% names(read_numbers))) {
    stop(paste0(
      "read_numbers must contain columns: ",
      paste(required_cols, collapse = ", ")
    ))
  }

  df <- read_numbers %>%
    dplyr::filter(
      (tissue == "Brain"  & stringr::str_starts(sample, "B")) |
      (tissue == "Kidney" & stringr::str_starts(sample, "K"))
    ) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      ont_unassigned = pmax(0, ont_fastq - ont_prim_aln),
      pb_unassigned  = pmax(0, pb_fastq  - pb_prim_aln),
      sample_label   = infer_sample_label(sample)
    )

  # Assemble per-technology rows
  pacbio_df <- df %>%
    dplyr::transmute(
      sample_label,
      technology    = "pacbio",
      prim_aln      = pb_prim_aln,
      unassigned    = pb_unassigned
    )

  ont_df <- df %>%
    dplyr::transmute(
      sample_label,
      technology    = "ont",
      prim_aln      = ont_prim_aln,
      unassigned    = ont_unassigned
    )

  dplyr::bind_rows(pacbio_df, ont_df) %>%
    tidyr::pivot_longer(
      cols = c("prim_aln", "unassigned"),
      names_to = "assign_category",
      values_to = "num_reads"
    ) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      assign_category = dplyr::recode(assign_category, prim_aln = "Aligned", unassigned = "Unaligned"),
      sample_label    = factor(sample_label, levels = paste(tissue, 1:5)),
      technology      = factor(technology, levels = c("pacbio", "ont")),
      assign_category = forcats::fct_relevel(assign_category, "Aligned", "Unaligned")
    )
}

plot_readnum_faceted <- function(read_numbers, tissue) {
  plot_mouse_readnum_faceted(build_readnum_counts(read_numbers, tissue), tissue)
}

# Render Brain and Kidney plots
brain_readnum_plot  <- plot_readnum_faceted(read_numbers, "Brain")
brain_readnum_plot
paper_ggsave(file.path(out_dir, "brain_readnum_plot.pdf"), plot = brain_readnum_plot, size = "mouse_10panel")

kidney_readnum_plot <- plot_readnum_faceted(read_numbers, "Kidney")
kidney_readnum_plot
paper_ggsave(file.path(out_dir, "kidney_readnum_plot.pdf"), plot = kidney_readnum_plot, size = "mouse_10panel")

```


```{r read_length_files_once, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}

lengths_base_dir <- paper_sqanti_lengths_root()

read_lengths_file <- function(file_path, sample_code, technology) {
  if (!file.exists(file_path)) return(tibble())
  vals <- readr::read_tsv(file_path, col_names = FALSE, show_col_types = FALSE)[[1]]
  tibble(
    length = as.numeric(vals),
    sample = paste(
      ifelse(substr(sample_code,1,1) == "B", "Brain", "Kidney"),
      as.integer(substr(sample_code, nchar(sample_code), nchar(sample_code)))
    ),
    technology = technology
  )
}

build_lengths_df <- function(dir_path) {
  ont_dir <- file.path(dir_path, "ont")
  pb_dir  <- file.path(dir_path, "pb")
  codes <- c(paste0("B",31:35), paste0("K",31:35))
  ont_files <- file.path(ont_dir, paste0(codes, ".bam.readlen.txt"))
  pb_files  <- file.path(pb_dir,  paste0(codes, ".bam.readlen.txt"))

  ont_df <- purrr::map2_dfr(ont_files, codes, ~ read_lengths_file(.x, .y, "ont"))
  pb_df  <- purrr::map2_dfr(pb_files,  codes, ~ read_lengths_file(.x, .y, "pacbio"))

  dplyr::bind_rows(ont_df, pb_df) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      sample = factor(sample, levels = c(paste("Brain",1:5), paste("Kidney",1:5))),
      technology = factor(technology, levels = c("pacbio","ont"))
    )
}

# Build once and reuse
all_lengths_df <- build_lengths_df(lengths_base_dir)

``` 


```{r plot_read_length_violin_funcs, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}

plot_lengths_violin_from_df <- function(lengths_df_raw, tissue) {
  paper_plot_lengths_violin(
    lengths_df_raw,
    tissue = tissue,
    title = tissue,
    show_technology_axis = TRUE
  )
}

```


```{r plot_read_length_violin_brain, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}

brain_lengths_violin  <- plot_lengths_violin_from_df(all_lengths_df, "Brain")
brain_lengths_violin
paper_ggsave(file.path(out_dir, "brain_lengths_violin.pdf"), plot = brain_lengths_violin, size = "mouse_10panel")

```


```{r plot_read_length_violin_kidney, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}

kidney_lengths_violin <- plot_lengths_violin_from_df(all_lengths_df, "Kidney")
kidney_lengths_violin
paper_ggsave(file.path(out_dir, "kidney_lengths_violin.pdf"), plot = kidney_lengths_violin, size = "mouse_10panel")

```


```{r combine_brain_plots, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}

brain_combined <- patchwork::wrap_plots(
  L = brain_sq_reads_plot + theme(legend.position = "right", plot.title = element_blank(), plot.margin = margin(r = 12)),
  R = brain_lengths_violin + theme(plot.title = element_blank(), plot.margin = margin(l = 12)),
  design = "LR"
)

brain_combined
paper_ggsave(file.path(out_dir, "brain_combined_plots.pdf"), plot = brain_combined, size = "read_level_combo")

```


```{r combine_kidney_plots, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}

kidney_combined <- patchwork::wrap_plots(
  L = kidney_sq_reads_plot + theme(legend.position = "right", plot.title = element_blank(), plot.margin = margin(r = 12)),
  R = kidney_lengths_violin + theme(plot.title = element_blank(), plot.margin = margin(l = 12)),
  design = "LR"
)

kidney_combined
paper_ggsave(file.path(out_dir, "kidney_combined_plots.pdf"), plot = kidney_combined, size = "read_level_combo")

```


```{r ref-tusco-sirv-annotation-index, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}

# Tissue-independent: ncbiRefSeq+SIRV lengths and GTF gene symbols (once per knit).
mouse_ref_annotation_lookup <- get_mouse_reference_annotation_lookup()

```


```{r ref-tusco-sirv-brain, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}

brain_ref_tusco_sirv_figure <- build_ref_tusco_sirv_tissue_figure(
  pacbio_dir = pacbio_dir,
  ont_dir = ont_dir,
  sample_codes = paste0("B", 31:35),
  sample_labels = paste("Brain", 1:5),
  tissue_label = "Brain",
  tusco_tissue = "brain",
  lookup = mouse_ref_annotation_lookup
)

paper_ggsave(
  file.path(out_dir, "brain_ref_tusco_sirv_violins.pdf"),
  plot = brain_ref_tusco_sirv_figure,
  size = "ref_tusco_sirv"
)

brain_ref_tusco_sirv_figure

```


```{r ref-tusco-sirv-kidney, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}

kidney_ref_tusco_sirv_figure <- build_ref_tusco_sirv_tissue_figure(
  pacbio_dir = pacbio_dir,
  ont_dir = ont_dir,
  sample_codes = paste0("K", 31:35),
  sample_labels = paste("Kidney", 1:5),
  tissue_label = "Kidney",
  tusco_tissue = "kidney",
  lookup = mouse_ref_annotation_lookup
)

paper_ggsave(
  file.path(out_dir, "kidney_ref_tusco_sirv_violins.pdf"),
  plot = kidney_ref_tusco_sirv_figure,
  size = "ref_tusco_sirv"
)

kidney_ref_tusco_sirv_figure

```


```{r source_data_capture, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# ---- Source Data capture: read-level (F3/SF1) + ref-TUSCO-SIRV (SF11/SF12) ---
# One figure = two sub-sheets.
#   F3/SF1 a: stacked structural-category read counts (already aggregated).
#   F3/SF1 b: read-length distribution. Raw per-read lengths run to millions of
#     rows (infeasible in .xlsx), so we report the summary statistics that
#     define the violin + box (n and five-number summary, nt), computed on the
#     same 99.5th-percentile-trimmed values shown in the figure.
#   SF11/SF12 a: transcript-length summary per set (nt).
#   SF11/SF12 b: expression summary per set x technology (figure panels b/c).
# Fragments persist to disk; write_source_data() assembles the workbook later.

# Per sample x technology read-length summary, mirroring the figure's trim.
.sd_length_summary <- function(lengths_df, tissue, trim_quantile = 0.995) {
  lengths_df %>%
    dplyr::filter(grepl(tissue, .data$sample)) %>%
    dplyr::group_by(.data$sample, .data$technology) %>%
    dplyr::filter(
      .data$length <= stats::quantile(.data$length, trim_quantile, na.rm = TRUE)
    ) %>%
    dplyr::summarise(
      n_reads   = dplyr::n(),
      min_nt    = min(.data$length),
      q25_nt    = stats::quantile(.data$length, 0.25),
      median_nt = stats::median(.data$length),
      mean_nt   = mean(.data$length),
      q75_nt    = stats::quantile(.data$length, 0.75),
      max_nt    = max(.data$length),
      .groups   = "drop"
    ) %>%
    as.data.frame()
}

# Ref/TUSCO/SIRV plotted frames are attached to the figure object (see R/10).
.sd_rts_length <- function(fig) {
  attr(fig, "source_data")$length %>%
    dplyr::group_by(.data$transcript_set) %>%
    dplyr::summarise(
      n_transcripts = dplyr::n(),
      min_nt    = min(.data$value),
      q25_nt    = stats::quantile(.data$value, 0.25),
      median_nt = stats::median(.data$value),
      mean_nt   = mean(.data$value),
      q75_nt    = stats::quantile(.data$value, 0.75),
      max_nt    = max(.data$value),
      .groups   = "drop"
    ) %>%
    as.data.frame()
}
.sd_rts_expression <- function(fig) {
  sd <- attr(fig, "source_data")
  dplyr::bind_rows(
    dplyr::mutate(
      summarize_ref_tusco_sirv_expression(sd$expression_pacbio),
      technology = "pacbio"
    ),
    dplyr::mutate(
      summarize_ref_tusco_sirv_expression(sd$expression_ont),
      technology = "ont"
    )
  ) %>%
    dplyr::relocate(technology, .after = transcript_set) %>%
    as.data.frame()
}

add_source_sheet("Fig 3a",
  "Figure 3a: number of reads per SQANTI3 structural category, per sample and technology (brain); 'Unaligned' = FASTQ reads minus SQANTI-assigned reads.",
  as.data.frame(category_counts_brain))
add_source_sheet("Fig 3b",
  "Figure 3b: read-length distribution per sample and technology (brain); summary statistics (nt) of the 99.5th-percentile-trimmed reads shown as violin + box plots.",
  .sd_length_summary(all_lengths_df, "Brain"))

add_source_sheet("Supplementary Fig 1a",
  "Supplementary Fig. 1a: number of reads per SQANTI3 structural category, per sample and technology (kidney).",
  as.data.frame(category_counts_kidney))
add_source_sheet("Supplementary Fig 1b",
  "Supplementary Fig. 1b: read-length distribution per sample and technology (kidney); summary statistics (nt) of the 99.5th-percentile-trimmed reads.",
  .sd_length_summary(all_lengths_df, "Kidney"))

add_source_sheet("Supplementary Fig 11a",
  "Supplementary Fig. 11a: transcript-length distribution by set (Reference/TUSCO/SIRV), brain; summary statistics (nt), Reference trimmed at its 99.5th percentile as in the figure.",
  .sd_rts_length(brain_ref_tusco_sirv_figure))
add_source_sheet("Supplementary Fig 11b",
  "Supplementary Fig. 11b: per-transcript expression (read counts) by set and technology (brain); catalog size, number of zero/positive transcripts, minimum positive, maximum, and total reads.",
  .sd_rts_expression(brain_ref_tusco_sirv_figure))

add_source_sheet("Supplementary Fig 12a",
  "Supplementary Fig. 12a: transcript-length distribution by set (Reference/TUSCO/SIRV), kidney; summary statistics (nt).",
  .sd_rts_length(kidney_ref_tusco_sirv_figure))
add_source_sheet("Supplementary Fig 12b",
  "Supplementary Fig. 12b: per-transcript expression (read counts) by set and technology (kidney).",
  .sd_rts_expression(kidney_ref_tusco_sirv_figure))
```
